title: Wholesome Bitter Polish Tofu Bowl
description: A crunchy, bitter tofu bowl with katsuobushi flakes, confit tomato, and buckwheat noodles that delivers an energizing, macrobiotic-friendly meal.
instructions: Press tofu for 15 minutes to remove excess moisture.
Cook buckwheat noodles according to package instructions, then drain and set aside.
Heat a non‑stick skillet over medium heat and pan‑fry tofu cubes until golden on all sides, about 4 minutes per side.
In a small bowl whisk together soy sauce, rice vinegar, chili flakes, and a splash of water to create a sauce.
Add the pre‑made confit tomato to the pan with tofu and pour in the sauce, simmering for 2 minutes.
Toss in shredded carrots and spinach, cooking until spinach wilts, about 1 minute.
Add the cooked soba noodles to the pan and stir to combine all ingredients.
Sprinkle katsuobushi flakes and sesame seeds over the bowl, then top with sliced spring onions.
Serve immediately while tofu remains crisp and noodles are al dente.
Optional: drizzle a little extra olive oil or a squeeze of lemon for brightness.
